reforms.description: The Swedish government has significantly increased the number of places in the education system, adding over 90,000 new positions from 1991 to 1995 to enhance educational attainment and support the labor market. Additionally, the 1995/96 Budget proposal included measures worth SKr 7 billion to create jobs, notably through temporary recruitment subsidies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing educational attainment and job creation supports long-term economic growth by enhancing the skills of the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms were executed and are reflected in the education system and labor market outcomes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant increase in educational positions and job creation measures represent a structural change in human capital policy.
reforms.source_quote: The 1995/96 Budget proposal also contained measures worth SKr 7 billion to create jobs, notably through temporary recruitment subsidies.

reforms.description: The Economic Policy Bill of November 1994 introduced allowances for investment in structures undertaken before May 1996, aimed at stimulating investment and job creation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Stimulating investment through allowances encourages economic activity and job creation, contributing to GDP growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The allowances were enacted and are in effect for qualifying investments.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is narrower in scope compared to major structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: The Economic Policy Bill of November 1994 introduced allowances for investment in structures undertaken before May 1996.

reforms.description: The Swedish government increased the availability of seed capital by injecting public funds into newly-established portfolio-investment companies and venture-capital companies to support small and medium-sized enterprises. Additionally, financial aid is offered to unemployed persons starting their own enterprises, aiming to stimulate entrepreneurship and job creation in the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting startups and entrepreneurship fosters innovation and job creation, which are vital for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives have been executed and are operational in supporting startups.
reforms.importance_rationale: The measures significantly enhance the startup ecosystem but are not as transformative as major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: The availability of seed capital was increased in 1992 by an injection of public funds of SKr 12 billion into two newly-established portfolio-investment companies and six new venture-capital companies.
